title: Ionization energy
definition: The energy needed to remove the most loosely held electron from a gas-phase atom: $\mathrm{X(g)} \rightarrow \mathrm{X^+(g)} + e^-$. First ionization energies generally climb across a period and fall down a group — the same pull that shrinks atoms holds their electrons — but the measured values keep two famous dips per period (the s→p boundary and the first paired p electron), which is why ordering drills must be answered from data, not from the slogan. Low ionization energy is what makes metals cation-formers.
